other_observers: Cole Wild, Dave Leatherman, Joe Mammoser, Steve Mammoser, Eric DeFonso, Dave and Sue Bolton, Darrel Fargo, Dix Smith, Jim Cruse, Connie Stickel, Soloman Miller and Elaine Coley.; Connie Kogler
specific_location: 4778 Hay Wagon Ct. Loveland, Co. 80537. My house, in my front yard.
description: This bird looked exactly like the adult interior bird in David Sibley's guide on page 481. The white eye-ring was distinctive. It had the pale gray brown head, throat stripe, white chin, buffy along the shoulders. Photos included.Behaviors: What first caught my eye about this bird was the way it ran like a thrasher, no hopping. It mingled with Juncos, a White-crowned Sparrow and an Am. Tree Sparrow. It would feed on the ground with them, eating millet, and frequently fly to my brush pile. It would also occasionally spend time in my aspen trees. This bird almost constantly flicked it's tail and held it high when running.Call: Not heardPlumage: Adult
photographer: Photographer: Connie Kogler, Connie Kogler, Connie Kogler
